mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production signals expanding economic output, which drives corporate earnings higher and lifts stock prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rolling 12-month correlation of INDPRO growth and 20-day forward returns remains below 0.1, or Rank IC over any 3-year window is less than 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate signals monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the near term.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS greater than 0.25% for two consecutive FOMC announcements
failure_condition: After FEDFUNDS exceeds 0.25%, 20-day forward returns are positive more than 50% of occurrences over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in housing starts and an increase in the federal funds rate indicates weakening residential investment compounded by restrictive monetary policy, signaling economic deceleration that negatively impacts equity markets.
candidate_trigger: HOUST declines more than 5% month-over-month and FEDFUNDS rises in the same month
failure_condition: The joint trigger produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of occurrences, or the signal has no significant predictive power (p>0.1).

mechanism_narrative: In a high inflation regime (CPI year-over-year above 3%), the Federal Reserve is forced to tighten policy, and any further rise in the federal funds rate amplifies the negative impact on equity prices through compressed valuations.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year change exceeds 3% and FEDFUNDS rate increases in the same month
failure_condition: In high inflation periods, the signal yields 20-day forward return accuracy below 50% or non-significant Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate falling below 4% signals an overheated labor market that will eventually lead to wage inflation and aggressive Fed tightening, causing negative equity returns over the following month.
failure_condition: After UNRATE drops below 4%, 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of cases over any 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth reflects robust consumer spending, which drives corporate earnings and lifts stock prices in the very short term as the market reacts to spending data.
failure_condition: Three-month moving average of RSAFS growth is not positively correlated with 5-day forward returns over any 3-year period (correlation < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production declines while employment continues to rise signals a weakening supply side masked by delayed labor indicators, foreshadowing a broader economic slowdown and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for two consecutive months while PAYEMS rises over the same period
failure_condition: The divergence signal is followed by negative 60-day forward returns in less than 60% of cases, or the signal's accuracy is not statistically significant (p>0.1).

mechanism_narrative: During post-recession recovery periods, accelerating GDP growth signals sustainable expansion which boosts corporate profits and drives positive equity market returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: In post-recession regimes, GDP growth is not positively correlated with 60-day forward returns (correlation <0.1) or the signal provides no excess return over a buy-and-hold strategy.
